description: Pre-Session B classification for shame (reg 146). Anchor confirmed: Heb 12:2. Phase 1 status = In Progress (three data files). Extracts H2616B cha.sad (3v, None-status). Extracts G1870 epaischunomai (be ashamed of, 14 occ, 0v) from candidate_delete — critical for the not-ashamed-of-Christ vocabulary (Rom 1:16, Heb 2:11). Confirms candidate_delete for bleed vocabulary. Notes pa.neh (face) sub-glosses as bleed. Notes phase1 incomplete status.

operations.description: Confirm anchor verse Heb 12:2. 'looking to Jesus, the founder and perfecter of our faith, who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising the shame (aischunēn), and is seated at the right hand of the throne of God.' Most concentrated NT shame statement: Jesus endured shame, despised it, was seated in glory. The two-forms structure applied to the cross.

H2616B cha.sad [to shame] → extracted. 3 verses. Pro 25:10 — 'lest he who hears you bring shame upon you, and your ill repute have no end' — the shame that comes from betraying confidence. 2Sa 22:26 / Psa 18:25 use cha.sad as God showing himself merciful to the merciful — the che.sed/cha.sad root covers both kindness and its opposite (shaming). Term extracts the shame-through-betrayal dimension.

set.status_note: Shame through betrayal of confidence. Pro 25:10 — ill repute from broken confidence.

operations.description: G1870 epaischunomai [be ashamed of] → extracted_thin. 14 occurrences, 0 verses in this export. Critical: Rom 1:16 (not ashamed of gospel), Mar 8:38/Luk 9:26 (ashamed of Jesus and words), Heb 2:11 (Jesus not ashamed to call them brothers), 2Ti 1:8,12,16 (not ashamed of testimony). The directed-shame vocabulary — shame as orientation toward Christ. Requires verse data from Claude Code.

set.status_note: Directed shame vocabulary: ashamed/not-ashamed of Christ/gospel. Requires verse data.

operations.description: Set all None-status terms without verses → delete. Bleed vocabulary: pa.neh face sub-glosses (H6440H/I/J/K/L/M/N — face entered via 'shame covered face' contexts but pa.neh itself is face not shame), para (Greek preposition), che.sed sub-glosses (H2617A primary in love; H2616A kindness), qa.lal (lighten/curse), pe.ni.mi/pe.ni.mah (inner/within), ba.ash (stink — primary in abomination registry), ka.saph (to long), cha.sad (H2616A kindness, distinct from H2616B extracted), be.esh, chag.ga, pa.nah Corner Gate.

set.exclusion_reason: Bleed vocabulary. No primary shame content.

operations.description: Confirm candidate_delete → delete for remaining 62 terms (all except G1870 epaischunomai, handled in OP-003). Key confirmations: G0821 atimoō (covered by G0818 atimazō extracted), H3001 ya.vesh (withering not shame), G5196 hubris (covered by G5195 hubrizō), G5092/G5093/G5091 timē/timios/timaō (honor vocabulary — shame's opposite but not shame itself), H7045 qe.la.lah (curse), H5953A a.lal (abuse), H2282/H2287 chag/cha.gag (feast vocabulary), H2658 cha.phar (to search — different from H2659 cha.pher already extracted).

set.exclusion_reason: Confirmed candidate_delete. Bleed or adequately covered.

operations.description: Phase 1 status = 'In Progress'. Three data files registered (parts 1-3, all Phase 1 STEP Data Assimilation status=None). This is noted for Claude Code. The Session B analysis proceeds with the 27 extracted terms (441 verses) that are present. The phase1 incomplete status does not block Session B — the active vocabulary is sufficient for a complete analysis. Any additional terms from incomplete Phase 1 processing will require a supplementary export when Phase 1 is closed.

set.session_b_note: Phase 1 In Progress — 3 data files pending. Session B analysis based on 27 extracted terms (441 verses). Supplement on Phase 1 completion.

new_extractions: G1870 epaischunomai (0v) — be ashamed of Christ
summary.phase1_note: Phase 1 = In Progress. Three data files registered but status=None. Session B proceeds with current 27 extracted terms.
summary.key_finding: G1870 epaischunomai — the not-ashamed-of-Christ vocabulary (Rom 1:16; Mar 8:38; Heb 2:11) — incorrectly in candidate_delete; extracted_thin, requires verse data.
